logline: A young sous-chef returns to her coastal hometown after her mother's sudden death, only to discover the family restaurant hides a generations-old secret recipe that her ruthless metropolitan employer will do anything to steal.
theme: Belonging vs. ambition — what we owe the places that made us.

characters.name: Lin Xiaoyao
characters.arc: Hot-headed metropolitan sous-chef → reconciles ambition with filial duty
characters.core_wound: abandoned her mother for a culinary career in Shanghai 5 years ago
characters.external_goal: win the Golden Wok national chef tournament

characters.name: Mr. Tan
characters.arc: Charismatic Shanghai restaurateur → revealed as recipe-thief + blackmailer
characters.core_wound: never created an original dish in his life
characters.external_goal: acquire the Lin family recipe by any means
characters.internal_goal: prove he belongs at the top of the culinary world

characters.name: Auntie Mei
characters.arc: Stubborn aunt → reveals the recipe's true (communal, not secret-sauce) nature
characters.core_wound: watched Xiaoyao leave and never forgave herself for not stopping her
characters.internal_goal: pass on the family's real legacy

setting.atmosphere: salt air, neon shrines, wet markets at dawn, scooter horns, religious incense
key_locations: Lin Family Restaurant — 40-year-old corner shop, neon sign half-burnt
Harbor dock — where Xiaoyao's mother kept her fishing boat
Ancestral kitchen — back room with a 100-year-old wood stove

story_spine.inciting_incident: Xiaoyao receives a voicemail from Auntie Mei: her mother drowned during a typhoon; the restaurant is hers now.
story_spine.central_question: Will Xiaoyao sell the restaurant to Mr. Tan and return to Shanghai, or stay and discover what her mother was actually protecting?
story_spine.act_1_turn: Xiaoyao finds a hidden recipe journal in the wood stove — written in code.
story_spine.midpoint_reversal: Mr. Tan arrives in the village, offers her 5 million RMB for the journal — revealing he has been hunting it for years.
story_spine.all_is_lost: Xiaoyao's Shanghai restaurant fires her; her metropolitan identity collapses.
story_spine.climax: Golden Wok finale: Xiaoyao cooks the family recipe on live TV — but公开 reveals it is not a secret sauce but a technique every fishing village family in Fujian uses. Mr. Tan's brand collapses.
story_spine.resolution: Xiaoyao reopens the restaurant with Auntie Mei, hosts a village-wide banquet.

content_safety: SFW; no on-screen death depiction; typhoon scene uses stock footage; culinary scenes use food-stylist props (no real animal slaughter)
